description:
Goal: run the IR interpreter from Compiler2-owned backend artifacts instead of old planner state. Signal: the Compiler2 interpreter path consumes the new backend IR + emission facts directly and never re-runs plan_module_with_role or resolve_module_types to fill gaps.

Change:
- Add a Compiler2 interpreter entry path that consumes the backend IR produced by fz-rh2.8.2.1.
- Remove Compiler2 interpreter dependence on ModulePlan planning/type-resolution fallback.
- Thread callable-entry, return-ABI, and extern facts from Compiler2 artifacts into the interpreter code image/runtime seam.
- Add runtime tests for quicksort, Enum.reduce, and variadic extern via the Compiler2 interpreter path.

Why:
- The interpreter is the smallest full consumer of the backend handoff and proves the lowered artifact surface is executable without planner rediscovery.

Checks:
- Compiler2 interpreter runs do not call plan_module_with_role or resolve_module_types.
- Runtime behavior matches the existing fixtures and artifact contracts.
title: Compiler2 backends: run the interpreter from Compiler2 artifacts
